que: What is JUnit commonly used for in Android projects?
Where are Android local unit tests placed by default?
Which annotation marks a JUnit test method?
Which assertion checks that two values are equal?
Where do local unit tests run?
options: On the development machine or server
Only on a physical Android device

que: What is a good characteristic of a local JUnit test?
options: It requires a full production backend
It must launch the whole app

que: What is a good practice for JUnit tests?
options: Keep each test focused on one behavior
Put many unrelated checks in one test
Skip assertions when the code compiles

que: When should instrumented tests be preferred over local JUnit tests?
options: When Android framework behavior must be tested on device
When writing pure Kotlin utility logic

que: What is a common mistake in basic testing?
options: Using vague test names that hide what failed
Testing one behavior at a time

que: What does a test method usually do?
options: Exercise code and verify the expected result
